subject: [PATCH v2 7/9] nfsd: fix fcache_disposal UAF by inlining dispose state into nfsd_net

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free (UAF) bug in the NFS daemon's file cache disposal mechanism by inlining the dispose state into the nfsd_net structure. The original code allocated a separate struct for the freeme queue, which was freed during per-net teardown, causing dangling pointers when accessed by other threads. By moving the spinlock and freeme list directly into the nfsd_net structure, the patch ensures that these fields have the same lifetime as the net namespace itself, preventing the UAF bug. The changes also simplify the code by removing alloc/free helpers and modifying the initialization and shutdown functions to work with the new inline fields.
